body.title: Processor emits a KEY=VAL env file so systemd/cron/launchers get UU_HOME_DB_URL
body.intention: I intend that non-interactive DB-connecting processes receive the composed env, so removing code fallbacks drops no connections.
body.description:
**Problem (from T-uu-env-coverage-audit):** the composed UU_HOME_DB_URL reaches only interactive shells; systemd --user units (ground_loop + supervised web/devices) and crons can't source the bash profile, so they rely on the hardcoded fallback. THE blocker for the connection-string sweep + password rotation. **Shape:** have the processor (or a vault refresh step) write a bare KEY=VAL env file at ~/.unseen_university/uu.env (UU_HOME_DB_URL, UU_ROOT, IGOR_NAME/INSTANCE_ID, CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS, ...); point ground_loop.service `EnvironmentFile=` at it; have DB-connecting crons source it. Extends the existing vault shell_env.cache pattern. **Affected files:** bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh (emit the file), the ground_loop systemd unit, crontab/Nanny cron wrappers. **Design rules:** secret-free repo (the env file is LOCAL, like uu_bash_profile.sh — chmod 600, not committed); CP6 recovery-safe (absent file = inherited/unset, never break). **Scope boundary:** IN — generate the env file + wire ground_loop + DB-connecting crons. OUT — the fallback removal itself (the sweep, gated on this); cron stale-PATH migration (launcher phase). **Test plan:** with .bashrc NOT sourced, a process started with the env file (e.g. `env -i bash -c 'set -a; . ~/.unseen_university/uu.env; python3 -c "import os;print(os.environ[\"UU_HOME_DB_URL\"])"')`) resolves the URL; ground_loop unit shows EnvironmentFile=. **Completion criteria:** ground_loop + DB-connecting crons resolve UU_HOME_DB_URL from the env file with no .bashrc and no code fallback.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

## 2026-06-24 — IGOR_HOME removed from scope
The env file does NOT carry IGOR_HOME — that env var is being eliminated (T-uu-eliminate-igor-home-env); the data dir is derived via uu_home(). The env file delivers UU_HOME_DB_URL, UU_ROOT, IGOR_NAME/INSTANCE_ID, CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS.
body.result: Processor emits ~/.unseen_university/uu.env (chmod 600, local, never committed) carrying UU_HOME_DB_URL + UU_ROOT + IGOR_SWARM_NAME + IGOR_INSTANCE_ID + CC_TMUX_SESSION + CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS as bare KEY='value' lines — proven sourceable (set -a; . uu.env) AND systemd-parseable (systemd-run EnvironmentFile: scheme=postgresql). ground_loop unit now reads it via EnvironmentFile=-%h/.unseen_university/uu.env, so GL's env (the root of the shim-launch tree) delivers the DB URL to web_server, scraps, Nanny, igor-when-pinged — the architecture-correct single delivery point, not a per-device sweep. daemon-reload done, no GL restart (live on next natural restart; hardcoded fallback still covers the gap until the sweep removes it). Nanny needs no separate wiring — her shim launches her with GL's env. Proof @ c370c65d. UNBLOCKS the connection-string sweep + password rotation.
